summary: First iteration E2E testing for Talentgram Portfolio Link Engine. Backend pytest suite (8 tests) all passed. Frontend Playwright E2E covering admin login -> talent create -> link generation -> client identify -> shortlist + comment also passed. Backend results API aggregated view_count=1, unique_viewers=1, shortlist=1, comment persisted correctly.

minor.issue: Requires admin auth. Client public flow fetches via /api/public/links/{slug} so this is fine, but consider confirming by design.
Does not check file size / type. Arbitrary mime accepted. Low priority but add guardrails for production.

ui_bugs.issue: Displays talent_id UUID (e.g. 'ef3010a8-fdda-...') instead of talent name in the per-talent breakdown row. Should join talent summary with talent name.

issues: Show talent name (and optionally thumbnail) rather than raw UUID in the breakdown row for better UX

action_items: Fix LinkResults talent breakdown: fetch talents by link.talent_ids and show talent.name (+ thumbnail) instead of talent_id UUID.
(Low) Add server-side validation for upload file size / MIME on POST /api/talents/{id}/media.
(Low) Consider adding rate limit on /api/public/links/{slug}/identify to prevent spam view inflation from a single email.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 692 lines — still single file. Consider splitting into routes/auth, routes/talents, routes/links, routes/public modules when adding more features.
GET /api/files/{path:path} is fully public by design for portfolio viewing — acceptable but document this; anyone who guesses a UUID path can read the object.

download-log only checks visibility.download. Consider also blocking if the talent_id is not in link.talent_ids (currently any viewer can log a download for any talent_id with viewer token for the slug).
link_views row is inserted on every identify call — unique_viewers is deduped by email but view_count grows on every re-identify. This matches the spec but worth calling out in analytics semantics.
No pagination on /api/talents (to_list(2000)) and /api/links — fine for MVP, add pagination if list grows.
ObjectId leakage is correctly suppressed via {'_id': 0} projections throughout — good.
test_credentials: admin@talentgram.com / Admin@123 (seeded). Viewer identity uses name+email; tested with QA Client / qa_client@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Playwright flow created one talent 'TEST_UI_Talent_QA' and one link 'Talentgram x QA Brand' (slug talentgram-x-qa-brand-7666b7) that were left in the DB for main agent to inspect. Backend pytest cleans up its own TEST_ prefixed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and covers auth, talents CRUD, media upload (uses 1x1 PNG inline bytes), link CRUD + duplicate, and full e2e flow including viewer identify/action/download-log + admin /results aggregation. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Frontend data-testids confirmed present on login, dashboard, talent list/edit, link generator, client view, detail overlay, action buttons. localStorage keys: 'tg_admin_token', 'tg_admin', 'tg_viewer_{slug}'. Known minor UI bug: LinkResults talent breakdown shows talent_id UUID instead of name.
rca of the issue: N/A — all tested flows working. The single UI polish issue (UUID instead of name in LinkResults breakdown) is because server returns summary with only talent_id; frontend doesn't join with talents list. Fix: in LinkResults.jsx, fetch the link's talents (already available via link.talent_ids) and render talents.find(t => t.id === summary.talent_id)?.name || summary.talent_id.
